steps.name: confirm-project-name-and-npm-availability
steps.summary: noteforge unscoped + scope @noteforge available on npm; proceed with rename

steps.name: rename-monorepo-to-final-name
steps.summary: monorepo renamed @obpub → @noteforge (root: noteforge), all imports/configs/docs synced; CLI bin obpub retained; lockfile regenerated; AC pass

steps.name: add-license-mit
steps.summary: MIT LICENSE added at root, license:MIT field added to 4 publishable packages + apps/blog, README link updated

steps.name: add-contributing-md
steps.summary: CONTRIBUTING.md added (dev env, TDD with privacy canaries, conventional commits, PR checklist, security reporting via GitHub Issues — dedicated email TBD)

steps.name: release-notes-and-readme-polish
steps.summary: CHANGELOG.md v0.1.0 created (Added/Known limitations/Acknowledgments), README polished with Pre-release naming + CHANGELOG link + screenshot placeholder, TODO.md Step 7 fully checked off; v0.1.0 git tag deferred to user manual command
steps.verification_notes: AC pass: typecheck/lint/vitest(389)/blog build all green. Dev server (pnpm --filter blog dev) bootstrap on the real 1.4GB /mnt/c Obsidian vault is dominated by primeFromVault() walk + WSL2 polling; primeFromVault completed at ~7m and content sync started, but port-bind exceeded the verification budget. The README pnpm command itself is correct — production build via the same core pipeline succeeds in 10s — slowness is environment-specific (WSL2 + large vault), not a doc bug. Verified the dev pipeline progresses through the documented stages.
